registry.description: Honesty is the inner commitment to correspondence between reality and what one says — speaking and acting in a way that does not misrepresent what is true. The Hebrew vocabulary here (tsedaqah, righteousness) is broader than honesty in the narrow sense, but the commitment to truthful speech is embedded throughout. Scripture treats honesty as a relational virtue: it honours the other person by giving them accurate access to reality. Dishonesty deforms relationship; honesty is one of the foundational conditions for genuine community.
